title: Client Rights in Court Proceedings
description: What rights does a law firm client have during court proceedings? Legal professional privilege, right to representation, access to case files, and protection in civil proceedings.
content:
Clients Have Rights – From the First Consultation to the Verdict

Law firm clients hold a range of rights both in relation to their lawyer and vis-à-vis the court and other participants in the proceedings. Understanding these rights enables active participation in the process and informed decision-making. Specter & Associates Law Firm takes every care to ensure each client is fully aware of them.

Legal Professional Privilege and Confidentiality

The fundamental right of every client is legal professional privilege. A lawyer is obliged to keep confidential all information received from the client in connection with handling the case. This obligation:

  • is absolute and does not expire after the engagement ends,
  • protects conversations, documents, and correspondence between client and lawyer,
  • cannot be waived without a court order even upon request by law enforcement,
  • applies to all staff of the firm, not only the lawyer themselves.

Right to Representation by a Lawyer

In civil proceedings, every party has the right to be represented by a lawyer. A legal representative:

  • prepares procedural pleadings and motions on behalf of the client,
  • represents the client at court hearings,
  • lodges appeals and other legal challenges,
  • conducts settlement negotiations with the opposing party.

The client may revoke or change their power of attorney at any time. The final decision on litigation strategy rests with the client.

Client Rights in Civil Proceedings

During court proceedings, the client – as a party to the case – enjoys the following rights:

  • Right to be heard – every party has the right to present their position, arguments, and evidence to the court.
  • Right of access to the case file – a party may inspect the case file, take notes, and obtain copies.
  • Right to appeal – unfavourable decisions can be challenged before higher courts.
  • Right to settlement – at any stage of the proceedings, the parties may reach a settlement and resolve the dispute without a judgment.
  • Right to information on case progress – the lawyer is obliged to keep the client regularly informed of developments in the proceedings.

Access to Case Files

Every party to proceedings has the right to inspect the case file at the court registry. Documents may be reviewed, photocopied, and transcribed. The lawyer may also obtain copies of documents on the client's behalf. In cases involving criminal proceedings or a risk to participants, the court may partially restrict access to the file.

Right to a Court and Fair Trial

The Constitution of the Republic of Poland and the European Convention on Human Rights guarantee every person the right to a fair trial. This means:

  • having the case heard by an independent and impartial court,
  • proceedings conducted within a reasonable time,
  • the right to mount a defence and present one's case,
  • access to the reasoning behind the court's decision.

Have questions about your rights in court proceedings? The lawyers at Specter & Associates Law Firm in Warsaw will clarify any doubts and ensure full protection of your rights at every stage of the process.
author: Harvey Specter
